title: galium multiflorum var. subalpinum
intro: Galium multiflorum var. subalpinum, also known as Alpine Bedstraw, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Galium genus and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is also rich in antioxidants and has been traditionally used to support digestive health, boost the immune system, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress. Alpine Bedstraw can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory conditions.
